workflow.name: Generate tailored CV and Cover Letter from job links

parameters.text:
=### ROLE
You are an expert Career Coach and Technical Copywriter specializing in high-conversion cover letters. Your goal is to bridge the gap between a candidate's specific achievements and a company's unique needs.

### CONTEXT
1. Job Description:
{{ $(Merge).item.json.output }}

2. Candidate Resume:
{{ $(Merge).item.json.text }}

### TASK
Write a bespoke, compelling cover letter that does not simply repeat the resume but tells a story of how the candidate's past success will solve the employer's future problems.

### GUIDELINES
- **Tone:** Professional yet authentic and energetic. Avoid "corporate speak" or "I am writing to apply for..." clichés.
- **The Hook:** Start with a strong opening statement that demonstrates immediate value or passion for the company’s mission.
- **Evidence-Based:** Select the top 2-3 achievements from the resume that directly map to the "Must-Haves" in the job description. Use metrics where available.
- **Cultural Fit:** Analyze the tone of the job description (e.g., startup vs. corporate) and mirror that language style.
- **Call to Action:** End with a confident closing that invites a conversation.

### CONSTRAINTS
- Length: Maximum 300 words.
- Format: Use standard professional letter formatting.
- No Hallucinations: Do not invent skills or experiences not found in the resume.
- Output: Return ONLY the text of the cover letter.

=### ROLE
You are a Professional Resume Writer and Applicant Tracking System (ATS) Optimization Expert. Your goal is to rewrite a candidate's CV to make it a perfect 95%+ match for a specific job description.

### INPUT DATA
- **Target Job Description:** {{ $(Merge).item.json.output }}
- **Current CV Data:** {{ $(Merge).item.json.text }}

### OBJECTIVE
Generate a tailored version of the CV that highlights the most relevant skills and experiences while maintaining 100% honesty.

### GUIDELINES & STRATEGY
1. **Keyword Optimization:** Identify the top 10 hard skills and tools mentioned in the Job Description. Ensure these are naturally integrated into the "Skills" section and "Professional Experience."
2. **Contextual Bullet Points:** Rewrite existing bullet points to lead with the results most relevant to the job. (e.g., If the job emphasizes Linux, move Linux-related achievements to the top of each role).
3. **Action Verbs:** Use strong action verbs (e.g., "Engineered," "Optimized," "Orchestrated") that match the seniority level of the job.
4. **Quantification:** Where the resume provides numbers (%, $, time), keep them. If a result is vague, refine the phrasing to sound more impactful without inventing data.
5. **Format:** Keep a clean, professional reverse-chronological structure.

### CONSTRAINTS
- **No Hallucinations:** Do not add certifications, degrees, or job titles that are not in the Current CV.
- **Tone:** Sharp, professional, and achievement-oriented.
- **Output:** Return ONLY the rewritten CV text. Do not include introductory remarks or "Here is your tailored CV."

nodes.name: If CV & Cover Letter not created
Get Web Data from Job Site
Extract Job Data from Job Site

parameters.content:
## How this works
* Everytime you add new JobLink into yout Google sheet ("Job IDs") this workflow will be triggered and new tailored CV and Cover Letter will be appended into Google sheet and also into Google Docs.

## How to set it up
**1 :** Create API keys for Google sheet [Link](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis), Google Docs [Link](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis) and OpenRouter [link](https://openrouter.ai/workspaces/default/keys/)
- If you do not know how create and add API key then simply search in google and you will fild solution or write comment in my YouTube video

**2 :** Create google sheet (Job IDs) and Docs (Cover Letter and CV) into your Google Drive and link them

**3 :** Learn how to use Webhook node and set it up so you can trigger it when you add new JobLink into Google sheet

## Constomization
* Replace to better free/paid AI model you like
* Edit promt to get better result
* Feel free to change workflow to get your desired output
## Triggered from Google sheet and get data from Google sheet
## Loop over each row from Google sheet
## Get data from your current CV

json.output:
**Dhaval Variya**  
Konstanzer Straße 18 • 79761 Waldshut, Germany  
Mobile: +49 157 5807 6904 • Email: variyadhaval2018@gmail.com  

---

### Professional Summary  
Motivated Embedded Software Engineer with hands‑on experience in C/C++ development for real‑time microcontroller platforms (ARM Cortex, STM32) and Linux‑based environments. Proven ability to design, integrate, and optimise firmware, device drivers and communication stacks (CAN, UART, SPI, I2C) for autonomous and safety‑critical systems. Strong background in automated testing, CI pipelines, and version‑controlled development (Git, Docker). Fluent in English (C1) and German (B2); ready to contribute to innovative projects in the Cologne area.

---

### Core Competencies  

| Embedded Development | Real‑Time Systems | Communication Interfaces |
|----------------------|-------------------|--------------------------|
| **C / C++ (bare‑metal & Linux)** | **RTOS integration (FreeRTOS, Zephyr)** | **CAN, UART, SPI, I²C** |
| **ARM Cortex‑M & STM32 MCUs** | **Interrupt handling & task scheduling** | **Protocol stack debugging** |
| **Device driver development** | **Deterministic timing analysis** | **V2X & automotive networking** |
| **Linux/Ubuntu, Yocto build** | **Peripheral configuration (ADC, PWM, Timers)** | **ROS 2 middleware** |
| **Git, GitHub, CI/CD (Jenkins)** | **Unit & integration testing (Unity, CMock)** | **REST‑API based tooling** |
| **Docker & containerisation** | **Memory optimisation (≤ 75 % footprint)** | **Automated test data compression** |
| **MATLAB/Simulink modelling** | **Performance profiling (perf, Trace32)** | **Sensor‑fusion algorithms** |
| **Python for tooling & scripts** | **Safety‑critical coding standards (MISRA‑C)** | **Documentation & traceability** |

---

### Professional Experience  

**Werkstudent – Software Development**  
*IAV GmbH, Ingolstadt, Germany* | 05 Jun 2023 – Present  
- Engineered embedded C/C++ modules for advanced driver‑assist functions on ARM‑based ECUs, adhering to MISRA‑C:2012 guidelines.  
- Integrated and validated CAN and UART communication drivers within a real‑time framework, reducing communication latency by **30 %**.  
- Designed and automated **continuous‑integration pipelines** (GitHub Actions, Docker) for unit‑ and integration‑tests, increasing test coverage to **92 %**.  
- Implemented a data‑compression utility that shrank test‑log files by **75 %**, cutting storage costs and archive times.  
- Developed a REST‑API wrapper for automatic file‑format conversion, decreasing manual processing effort by **90 %**.  

**Driverless Team Member – Autonomous Systems**  
*Schnaizer Racing Electric e.V., Ingolstadt, Germany* | 01 Apr 2024 – 15 Mar 2025  
- Programmed low‑level STM32 firmware in C++ for sensor interfacing (LiDAR, IMU) and actuator control via CAN and SPI.  
- Integrated ROS 2 nodes with real‑time Linux (PREEMPT_RT) to enable deterministic data handling for autonomous navigation.  
- Conducted hardware‑in‑the‑loop (HIL) and software‑in‑the‑loop (SIL) testing, identifying and fixing **12 critical timing bugs**.  
- Coordinated cross‑functional testing in Gazebo simulation and on‑track trials, achieving a **20 %** improvement in lane‑keeping accuracy.  

**Student Assistant – Research & Development**  
*Fraunhofer Institute, Ingolstadt, Germany* | 01 Jun 2022 – 31 May 2023  
- Developed Python scripts to automate solder‑joint inspection using machine‑vision, boosting defect detection rate to **98 %**.  
- Designed PCB layouts in Eagle for custom test rigs and performed thermal impedance measurements on power modules.  
- Assisted in configuring I²C and SPI peripherals for high‑speed data acquisition, reducing acquisition time by **35 %**.  

**Project Lead – VDI Challenge Autonomous Driving**  
*Technical University of Ingolstadt* | 15 Mar 2024 – 30 Sep 2024  
- Led a 5‑member team to create a ROS 2‑based autonomous vehicle prototype. Implemented sensor fusion (LiDAR, camera) and a lane‑detection algorithm in C++.  
- Developed CAN‑based actuator commands and integrated a custom RTOS scheduler to guarantee sub‑10 ms control loops.  
- Delivered a fully functional demonstration vehicle that autonomously followed road curvature with **±5 cm** lateral error.  

---

### Education  

**M. Ing. – KI Engineering & Autonomous Systems**  
Technical University of Ingolstadt, Germany | Expected 14 Mar 2026  

**B. Ing. – Autonomous Vehicle Technology**  
Technical University of Ingolstadt, Germany | 27 Feb 2025  
- GPA: 2.0  
- Relevant coursework: Embedded Systems, Digital Signal Processing, Vehicle Electronics, Real‑Time Operating Systems, V2X Communication, Machine Learning for Autonomous Driving.  

---

### Technical Projects  

- **UAV Navigation without GPS** (2025‑Present) – Integrated transformer‑based deep‑learning models into embedded C++ flight control software; achieved **15 %** navigation error reduction in GPS‑denied environments.  
- **Cross View Transformer Optimization** – Refactored Python‑heavy vision pipeline into C++/CUDA modules for edge devices, cutting inference time from 120 ms to 45 ms.  

---

### Tools & Languages  

- **Programming:** C, C++ (14/17), Python, Java, C#, MATLAB, R, TensorFlow, PyTorch, CUDA  
- **Embedded:** ARM Cortex‑M, STM32, FreeRTOS, Zephyr, Yocto, Linux (Ubuntu), ROS 2, CAN, UART, SPI, I²C, MBSE  
- **DevOps:** Git, GitHub, Docker, Jenkins, Jira, Jira, CI/CD, Unit testing (Unity, CMock)  
- **Simulation & Modelling:** Gazebo, MATLAB/Simulink, ROS, V2X simulators  

---

### Languages  

- **German:** B2 (professional working proficiency)  
- **English:** C1 (full professional proficiency)  
- **Gujarati:** Native  

---

### Additional Information  

- **Driver’s License:** Class B  
- **Interests:** Badminton, traveling, robotics competitions  

---  

*References available on request.*

Dhaval Variya  
Konstanzer Straße 18  
79761 Waldshut, Deutschland  
(+49) 157 58076904  
variyadhaval2018@gmail.com  

30 April 2026  

Amin Zada  
dotChange GmbH  
Köln, Deutschland  

Liebe Amin Zada,

wenn ich an „Bare‑Metal‑Entwickler“ denke, sehe ich sofort den Moment, in dem ein Mikrocontroller vom ersten Funktions‑Sketch zur stabilen Produktions‑Firmware wird – und genau hier habe ich die letzten Jahre meine größten Erfolge gefeiert.  

Bei IAV entwickelte ich ein automatisiertes Komprimierungs‑Tool, das die Größe von Testdatendateien um 75 % reduzierte und über eine REST‑API den manuellen Aufwand um 90 % senkte. Die Lösung war rein in C/C++ implementiert, lief direkt auf den Ziel‑Boards und wurde in die Continuous‑Integration‑Pipeline integriert – ein perfektes Beispiel dafür, wie ich Code‑Qualität, Performance und Tool‑Chain‑Integration gleichzeitig optimiere.  

Im Formula‑Student‑Team von Schnazer Racing Electric habe ich die autonome Fahrsoftware für ein STM32‑basiertes Fahrzeug entwickelt. Dort verantwortete ich die Anbindung von CAN, UART und SPI‑Schnittstellen und implementierte eine Echtzeit‑Aufgabensteuerung mit FreeRTOS. Unsere Tests in Gazebo und anschließend im realen Fahrzeug zeigten eine fehlerfreie Kommunikation und eine 30 % schnellere Reaktionszeit bei Fahrspur‑Erkennung.  

Ihre Mission, abwechslungsreiche Projekte in einem technologisch innovativen Umfeld zu realisieren, trifft genau meine Passion, komplexe Embedded‑Systeme von Grund auf zu gestalten und kontinuierlich zu verbessern. Meine strukturierte, selbstständige Arbeitsweise und meine Erfahrung mit RTOS, Kommunikationsprotokollen und automatisierten Tests passen exakt zu Ihrem Anforderungsprofil.  

Ich freue mich darauf, in einem kurzen Gespräch zu zeigen, wie ich Ihre nächsten Bare‑Metal‑Projekte beschleunigen kann. Lassen Sie uns gemeinsam die nächste Generation von Embedded‑Lösungen für Köln entwickeln.

Herzliche Grüße,

Dhaval Variya

user.name: Dhaval Variya
user.bio: I'm Dhaval Variya, an AI & Software Engineer with a passion for clean code and workflow automation. I leverage my expertise in Python, Node.js, and DevOps tools like n8n and Docker to build efficient microservices and CI/CD pipelines. I have a proven track record of using REST APIs to automate processes and reduce manual data workloads by up to 90%. I love combining AI integration (OpenAI/LangChain) with n8n to build smarter, time-saving workflows.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
